compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: RESOLVED via PR #31 (squash-merged to main, commit on main). Root cause: inbound differ read the wrong snapshot key — jira_fields.get('comments') (flat) instead of the Jira REST nested jira_fields['comment']['comments'] that the fetcher enriches via get_comment_map. Fix: _diff_comments_inbound now reads the nested 'comment' key, mirroring outbound_differ, with absent/malformed guards; all echo/dedup/ADF/blank behavior preserved. Tests: added test_inbound_differ_comment_snapshot_shape.py (RED before fix, mutation-checked) and corrected the fictional flat-shape fixtures in test_inbound_comments_diff.py. Live-verified on REB-5: with_comments went 0 -> 1. Note: the ticket's original 'needs a client/fetch' fix approach was moot — snapshot already carries comment data under 'comment'; this was purely a read-key bug.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Completion verification result: **PASS** — all five acceptance criteria are met and the root-cause bug is fixed.

**Evidence summary:**

1. **AC1 / Bug fix — Jira-origin comments mirrored inbound:**
   `_diff_comments_inbound` (inbound_differ.py:352–421) now reads `jira_fields.get("comment")` → `comment_field.get("comments")` (the Jira REST nested key), mirroring the outbound differ. The old flat key `jira_fields.get("comments")` is gone. The fetcher enriches snapshots under `snapshot[key]["comment"]` (fetcher.py:417), which is exactly what this function now reads.

2. **AC2 / Simultaneous case converges:**
   Echo filtering (RECONCILER_MARKER check at line 409) and dedup by `jira_comment_id` set-diff (lines 391–406) together prevent double-import and echo re-import in the bidirectional case.

3. **AC3 — Locally-originated comments not re-imported:**
   Both guards are in place: `RECONCILER_MARKER in body_text` (line 409) and `jid_str in known_ids` from the `jira_comment_id` set (line 405).

4. **AC4 — `with_comments` no longer structurally 0:**
   reconcile.py:1041 counts `sum(1 for m in inbound_new if getattr(m, "comments", []))`. Since `_diff_comments_inbound` now returns actual mutations when comment data is present (instead of always returning `[]`), this counter will reflect real Jira-side comments.

5. **AC5 — RED→GREEN test:**
   `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_inbound_differ_comment_snapshot_shape.py` is present with no skip/xfail markers and exercises: (a) `test_diff_reads_real_comment_snapshot_shape` — Jira-origin comment yields an inbound add; (b) `test_diff_real_shape_filters_echo` — reconciler echo is not re-imported; (c) `test_compute_inbound_mirrors_jira_origin_comment` — full end-to-end path via `compute_inbound_mutations`.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. Pick a bound ticket with 0 comments on both sides (e.g. REB-5 / local
   0241-c6c1-0a20-491b).
2. Add a LOCAL comment: `rebar comment <local_id> "local-origin"`.
3. Add a JIRA comment to the same issue: `acli jira workitem comment create
   --key <REB-N> --body "jira-origin"`.
4. Run a reconcile pass (`rebar reconcile --mode live`).

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** bidirectional, additive comment sync — both sides end with BOTH
  comments (2 in Jira, 2 in local).
- **Actual:** **Jira = 2 (outbound works), local = 1 (inbound BROKEN).** The
  Jira-origin comment is never mirrored to local. `RECON: inbound_differ total=N
  with_comments=0` — zero inbound comment imports, fleet-wide (not just this ticket).

## Root cause (confirmed in code)

- `inbound_differ.compute_inbound_mutations(jira_snapshot, binding_store,
  local_by_id, outbound_mutations)` takes **no client**.
- `inbound_differ._diff_comments_inbound(jira_fields, local_ticket)` reads comments
  from `jira_fields["comment"]` — but the live **search snapshot does NOT include
  comment data** (Jira search omits comments; documented in `outbound_differ`). So the
  inbound differ sees 0 Jira comments and imports none.
- The OUTBOUND differ DOES fetch comments (`client.get_comments`, for dedup) but that
  fetched data is not shared with the inbound path. So outbound comment sync works and
  inbound does not.

## Fix approach

Give the inbound comment path the Jira comments — e.g. (a) thread the outbound
differ's already-fetched comments into the inbound snapshot/`compute_inbound_mutations`,
or (b) pass a client to `compute_inbound_mutations` so `_diff_comments_inbound` can
fetch via `get_comments` (bounded, mirroring the outbound path), or (c) include comment
data on the snapshot entries the inbound differ sees (reuse the absent-alive GET path).
Outbound comments carry the `<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->` marker and dedup is by
`jira_comment_id`, so our own echoes are already filtered — the fix must not re-import
locally-originated comments.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A comment created directly in Jira (no reconciler marker, not already in local)
      is mirrored inbound as a local comment on the next pass.
- [ ] The simultaneous case converges: 1 local + 1 Jira comment on the same ticket ->
      2 comments in local AND 2 in Jira (no duplicates, no echo re-import).
- [ ] Locally-originated comments (carrying the echo marker / matching jira_comment_id)
      are NOT re-imported.
- [ ] `inbound_differ ... with_comments` reflects real Jira-side comments (no longer
      structurally 0 when Jira-side comments exist).
- [ ] RED->GREEN test: a Jira-origin comment yields an inbound add; an echo does not.
compiled_state.title: Inbound comment sync broken: Jira-side comments never mirror to local (inbound differ has no comment data)
